reasoning: The base rate of the World Health Organization (WHO) declaring a pandemic is extremely low, occurring approximately once every 10-15 years. With only 22 days remaining in the forecasting window (June 8 to June 30, 2025), the expected probability based purely on historical frequency is under 0.5%. The most recent market consensus on Polymarket traded at exactly 2% just prior to the end of May. A rigorous search of recent global health news reveals no imminent plans or epidemiological triggers for the WHO to declare a new pandemic. Although Avian Influenza (H5N1) and Mpox are being closely monitored and have triggered expert warnings and Public Health Emergencies of International Concern (PHEICs), they do not currently demonstrate the sustained, widespread human-to-human transmission globally required to meet the WHO's strict criteria for a "pandemic." The 78th World Health Assembly in May 2025 heavily focused on signing a new "Pandemic Agreement" to prepare for future threats rather than managing a newly declared immediate pandemic. Given the lack of escalating signals over the past month and the very short timeframe remaining, the probability remains firmly at the market baseline.
